seoTitle: Bexley vs Biala — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Bexley and Biala. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bexley and Biala.
heading: Bexley vs Biala — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bexley, New South Wales and Biala, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Biala has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to Bexley (44/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bexley or Biala safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bexley has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Biala scores 30/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bexley scores 47/100 for bushfire risk. Biala scores 24/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bexley or Biala?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Biala has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.08% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
